seo.metaTitle: Landscape Fabric for Nurseries | DOTDAY SHIELD
seo.metaDescription: SHIELD 3.2oz woven landscape fabric for nurseries: chemical-free weed control that stays permeable so pots drain, built for container yards and pathways.

ogImage.alt: Landscape fabric for nurseries: DOTDAY SHIELD 3.2oz woven ground cover in a container yard

sections.heading: Landscape Fabric for Nurseries
sections.subheading: SHIELD is the 3.2oz woven ground cover built for nursery container yards, pot pads, and pathways. It blocks weeds without chemicals, stays permeable so pots drain instead of sitting in water, and gives a clean, stable surface that holds up to cart and foot traffic across a full-sun yard.

image.alt: Landscape fabric for nurseries: SHIELD 3.2oz woven ground cover laid across a container yard

caption.label: SHIELD 3.2oz in a container yard
caption.context: Drains so pots stay out of standing water

sections.heading: Why a container yard gets weedy, muddy, and slow
sections.body: In a nursery, the ground between and under your pots is working ground, and bare soil or loose gravel costs you. Weeds come up between containers and someone has to pull them. Poor-draining ground leaves pots sitting in water and roots suffering. And cheap fabric frays at the cut and breaks down after a season of full sun and cart traffic, so you are relaying it before the stock turns over.
bullets: Weeds between pots. Constant hand-weeding across the whole yard.
Standing water. Pots in poor-draining ground stress roots and stock.
Mud and instability. Bare ground ruts and shifts under carts and foot traffic.
Cheap fabric. Frays and degrades under UV before the season is out.

sections.heading: SHIELD is breathable weed control built for a working yard
sections.body: SHIELD is 100% woven virgin polypropylene at 3.2oz. The tight weave blocks the light weeds need while staying permeable, so water drains through and pots never sit in standing water. It is substantial enough to take cart wheels, foot traffic, and a full season of sun, the heat-sealed edges cut clean without fraying, and the printed 12-inch guides make it easy to lay out straight, even rows of pots and blocks.

image.alt: SHIELD landscape fabric for nurseries with printed 12-inch guides for straight rows

sections.heading: One woven fabric that does two jobs at once
sections.intro: SHIELD's tight woven face blocks the light weeds need, while the same weave stays open enough for water and air to pass through, weed control and free drainage from a single ground cover.

cards.title: Woven face blocks light
cards.body: The tight polypropylene weave stops sunlight reaching the soil, so weeds can't establish between or under your pots. Printed 12-inch guides keep rows of stock straight and evenly spaced.

image.alt: Tight woven SHIELD fabric blocking light to stop weeds in a nursery

cards.title: Permeable weave drains
cards.body: Unlike plastic or poor-draining ground, the weave lets water move straight through, so pots drain freely and never sit in standing water, and the soil below keeps breathing.

image.alt: Water draining through permeable SHIELD landscape fabric under nursery pots

sections.heading: SHIELD goes down in three steps

steps.title: Roll it out
steps.body: Lightweight woven cover unrolls fast across beds, pads, and pathways. Line the printed 12-inch guides up for straight rows.

steps.title: Cut to fit
steps.body: Trims clean with scissors or a knife, and the heat-sealed edges do not fray. Notch around pots and posts as needed.

steps.title: Secure and done
steps.body: Pin the edges and seams, set your stock back on top, and the surface stays clean, stable, and free-draining all season.

sections.heading: SHIELD Spec Sheet
sections.intro: Real, ASTM-tested attributes, not vague marketing claims. These are what matter on a nursery floor.
sections.footnote: Engineered to ASTM standards. SHIELD provides chemical-free weed control and is safe around plants and stock. Fabric size range 3 ft up to 8 ft.

sections.heading: SHIELD vs the alternatives for a container yard

items.quote: This stuff is really impressive, I should have tried this years ago. The plants have been in the ground for just under a month and the only weeds are at the outer edges, with the entire covered area absolutely weed free. Watering is a breeze: the water sits briefly on the surface but drains through to the dirt within a couple of minutes.
items.author: Patrick Quinn

items.q: What is the best landscape fabric for a nursery?
items.a: A breathable woven ground cover that blocks weeds while letting water drain. SHIELD is a 3.2oz woven polypropylene fabric built for nursery container yards, pot pads, and pathways, with 12-inch guides for straight rows.

items.q: Will SHIELD let my pots drain?
items.a: Yes. The woven structure stays permeable, so water passes straight through and pots never sit in standing water, which keeps roots healthy.

items.q: Does SHIELD hold up to cart and foot traffic?
items.a: Yes. At 3.2oz it is substantial enough to take cart wheels and daily foot traffic across a container yard, and the heat-sealed edges cut clean without fraying.

items.q: Is SHIELD safe around plants and stock?
items.a: Yes. It provides chemical-free weed control and is safe around plants, vegetables, and crops while letting air, water, and nutrients through.

items.q: How long does SHIELD last in a full-sun yard?
items.a: It is UV-stabilized for long outdoor life and typically lasts 5 to 10 years under moderate conditions, longer when shaded or covered.

items.q: SHIELD vs black plastic for a container yard, which is better?
items.a: SHIELD. Plastic pools water and suffocates the ground, while SHIELD blocks weeds and stays breathable, so pots drain and the surface stays stable.

items.q: How much does ground cover fabric for a nursery cost?
items.a: SHIELD rolls run from $59.98, with a $10.99 sample to test first and free shipping over $50. Nurseries can request bulk pricing and custom widths for whole-yard quantities.

items.q: Do you offer bulk pricing and custom widths for nurseries?
items.a: Yes. We supply nurseries direct, with custom widths for bulk orders. Request a quote with your yard size and we will spec it.

sections.heading: Building or re-laying a nursery yard?
sections.body: Tell us the area, the stock, and the traffic, and we'll recommend the right SHIELD width and the exact quantity. Request a quote or talk to us, practical answers from people who supply growing operations.
